INSU_CUSTOMER.instructions:
Purpose: Master records for insurance customers (individuals or businesses).
    customer_id: generate UUID string; must be unique.
    customer_type: choose from ['INDIVIDUAL','BUSINESS'] with distribution ~85/15.
    gender: if customer_type='INDIVIDUAL', choose from ['MALE','FEMALE','OTHER'] with distribution ~49/49/1; if 'Business', NULL.
    first_name/last_name: use faker names only when customer_type='INDIVIDUAL'; else 'NOT APPLICABLE'.
    business_name: faker.company() only when customer_type='BUSINESS'; else 'NOT APPLICABLE'.
    dob: if customer_type='INDIVIDUAL', random date between 1960-01-01 and 2005-12-31 (adults); if 'Business', NULL.
    email: faker.email(); ~100% populated; prefer global uniqueness.
    phone: faker.phone_number(); ~100% populated.
    country: pick from ['US','IN','AE','UK','CA'] with distribution ~50/20/10/10/10; state/city/postal coherent to country.
    address_line1: faker.street_address(); address_line2.
    registration_date: date between 2015-01-01 and current_date.
    created_at: datetime between registration_date and now (ensure created_at >= registration_date).

INSU_AGENT.instructions:
Purpose: Master records for licensed agents/brokers.
    agent_id: UUID; unique.
    agent_code: string like 'AGT' + 7–9 digits; must be unique.
    first_name/last_name: faker names.
    email: faker.email(); ~100% populated; unique-ish.
    phone: faker.phone_number(); ~100% populated.
    license_no: string like 'LIC' + 8–10 digits; ~98% unique.
    country/state/city: coherent geography; bias toward ['US','IN','UK'].
    status: choose from ['ACTIVE','INACTIVE'] with ~85/15.
    hire_date: between 2010-01-01 and today.
    created_at: datetime between hire_date and now.

INSU_POLICY.instructions:
POLICY_ID → Generate uuid4(); must be unique.
POLICY_NUMBER → Unique string like PL{YYYY}{8–10 digits} (e.g., PL2023 001829374).
CUSTOMER_ID → Pick an existing INSU_CUSTOMER.CUSTOMER_ID (NOT NULL).
PRODUCT_ID → Pick an existing INSU_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID, enforcing policy_type mix Auto/Home/Life/Health = 40%/25%/20%/15% via the product.
AGENT_ID → Pick an existing INSU_AGENT.AGENT_ID ~90% of the time, else NULL (direct/online sale).
STATUS → One of ['ACTIVE','LAPSED','CANCELED','EXPIRED'] with ~70% Active (rest distributed).
EFFECTIVE_DATE → Random date between 2016-01-01 and today.
EXPIRATION_DATE → EFFECTIVE_DATE + 1 year ± 60 days (ensure >= EFFECTIVE_DATE).
PREMIUM_BILLING_FREQ → One of ['MONTHLY','QUARTERLY','ANNUAL'] with ~70% Monthly (20% Quarterly, 10% Annual).
PREMIUM_AMOUNT → Annual premium > 0; suggest ranges by policy_type (Auto 300–2000, Home 500–3000, Life 200–5000, Health 200–4000) with ±15% noise; round 2 decimals.
CURRENCY → Choose from ['USD','INR','AED','GBP','CAD'], default from customer country 80–90% of time (10–20% cross-currency noise).
CREATED_AT → Timestamp between EFFECTIVE_DATE and LEAST(EXPIRATION_DATE, NOW()).

INSU_RISK_OBJECT.instructions:
Purpose: Insured risk objects linked to policies (vehicles, properties, persons, equipment).
    object_id: UUID; unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id; ~1–2 risk objects per policy on average to reach target rows.
    object_type: choose from ['VEHICLE','PROPERTY','PERSON','EQUIPMENT'] with ~50/35/10/5.
    description: faker.sentence(4–10 words).
    year_made: for Vehicle/Equipment, integer between 1980 and 2025;
    serial_no: ~100% populated for VEHICLE/PROPERTY;
    address_line1/address_line2/city/state/postal_code/country: mostly for Property (~80% filled when object_type='PROPERTY'); for other types, fill ~20–30% only.
    sum_insured: if available, pick between linked product.coverage_min and product.coverage_max; otherwise 20000–2000000.
    created_at: datetime between policy.effective_date and policy.expiration_date (or now if expiration in past).

INSU_CLAIM.instructions:
Purpose: Claims raised against policies.
    claim_id: UUID; unique.
    claim_number: string like 'CL' + 9–12 digits; must be unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id.
    customer_id: FK to INSU_CUSTOMER.customer_id (should match policy.customer_id).
    claim_type: choose from ['ACCIDENT','THEFT','FIRE','MEDICAL','NATURAL DISASTER','LIABILITY','OTHERS'] with ~45/10/8/15/7/10/5.
    incident_date: date between policy.effective_date and min(policy.expiration_date, today).
    reported_date: incident_date + 0–30 days (>= incident_date).
    status: choose from ['OPEN','IN-REVIEW','APPROVED','REJECTED','CLOSED','WITHDRAWN'] with ~25/20/25/10/18/2.
    loss_estimate: 500–500000 with long-tail; ensure >= 0.
    deductible: 0–5000; often <= product.deductible_max if accessible.
    approved_amount: if status in ['Approved','Closed'] then 50%–100% of loss_estimate; if 'Rejected' then 0; if others then NULL.
    closed_date: only when status='Closed'; pick between reported_date and reported_date + 365 days; else NULL.
    created_at: datetime is now.

INSU_PAYMENT.instructions:
Purpose: Monetary transactions for policies and claims (premiums, payouts, refunds).
    payment_id: UUID; unique.
    policy_id: FK to INSU_POLICY.policy_id.
    claim_id: optional FK to INSU_CLAIM.claim_id; present for payouts (~30%), NULL for premium payments (~65%) and policy refunds (~5%).
    direction: choose from ['Premium','Payout','Refund'] with ~65/30/5 (must align with claim_id presence).
    status: choose from ['Completed','Pending','Failed'] with ~90/7/3.
    method: choose from ['ACH','Card','Check','Wire','Cash','UPI'] with ~35/30/10/15/5/5.
    amount: > 0; random amount from  policy.premium_amount ±20% 
    currency: pick based on policy/customer country mapping ['USD','INR','AED','GBP','CAD'].
    payment_date: if claim_id present, between claim.reported_date and (claim.closed_date or claim.reported_date + 60 days); else within [policy.effective_date .. policy.expiration_date].
    transaction_ref: string like 'TX' + 10–14 digits; mostly unique.
    created_at: datetime between payment_date and now.
